name: Al-Arqam Islamic School
mission: TO MEET THE EDUCATIONAL NEEDS OF THE MUSLIM CHILDREN LIVING WITHIN THE GREATER SACRAMENTO AREA.
theoryOfChange: Al-Arqam Islamic School has published its 'Student Learning Goals' as part of its Islamic Education curriculum. These goals serve as a results framework, outlining the intended impact on students across five key areas: living Islam, becoming lifelong learners, effective communicators, citizens of the world, and honoring themselves physically and spiritually.
scoreSummary: Al-Arqam Islamic School shows exceptional impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC)
Accreditation (Western Association of Schools and Colleges (WASC))

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

score_details.score_summary: Al-Arqam Islamic School shows exceptional impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: The donation page for Al-Arqam Islamic School (Sacramento, CA) lists methods for donations (checks, PayPal, Zelle) but does not explicitly mention "zakat" or have a dedicated zakat fund or campaign. (Source: https://www.alarqamislamicschool.org/apps/pages/index.jsp?uREC_ID=1440031&type=d)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported a program expense ratio of 98.1% and total revenue of $4,330,166.
Financial records indicate a fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised and 9.1 months of working capital.
The school offers college preparatory academics and an International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Programme.
The organization operates as Al-Arqam Islamic School & College Preparatory.
The school maintains a student honor code and specific parent policy guidelines for the 2025-2026 academic year.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a 67/100 due to its excellent financial efficiency and specialized educational capacity, though its overall score is moderated by a lower alignment for general Muslim donor priorities compared to urgent humanitarian causes.
areas_for_improvement: Limited data regarding long-term post-graduation outcomes and specific impact metrics.
Low alignment for donors seeking high-urgency global humanitarian interventions.

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the school serves a vital role in the local Sacramento Muslim community, it has a lower alignment score for general donors because its focus is localized education rather than urgent global needs [1]. As a Sadaqah-eligible institution, it provides a consistent track record of service but lacks the broad appeal of high-urgency cause areas [2][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The school demonstrates high effectiveness in capacity building by dedicating 98.1% of its $4,330,166 total revenue to its educational programs [1][2]. Its academic structure, including an Honor Code and rigorous admissions standards, suggests a high-quality learning environment, though specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not available [3][6].

baseline_narrative.headline: Al-Arqam Islamic School provides rigorous college preparatory and International Baccalaureate education to Muslim youth in the Greater Sacramento area.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 98.1%, ensuring nearly all revenue directly supports student education.
Offers the prestigious International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Programme for advanced college preparation.
Strong financial stability with 9.1 months of working capital available.
baseline_narrative.summary: Al-Arqam Islamic School & College Preparatory is dedicated to meeting the educational needs of Muslim children through a curriculum that includes the International Baccalaureate (IB) Diploma Programme [3][6]. The institution operates with a strong focus on academic excellence and Islamic values, maintaining a high program expense ratio to support its core educational mission [1][2].

case_against.summary: The primary limitation is the <cite id="14">lack of public data</cite> regarding specific student outcome metrics and long-term impact.

confidence.notes: Financial data is highly reliable, but impact data is limited to accreditation status.

alignment.explanation: The school is a strong fit for <cite id="8">Muslim donors</cite> prioritizing local educational infrastructure and the preservation of Islamic identity in youth.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be improved by <cite id="17">expanding services to underserved populations</cite> or offering more robust financial aid transparency.

credibility.explanation: The school demonstrates high credibility through <cite id="11">100% board independence</cite> and a long <cite id="1">28-year track record</cite> in the Sacramento community.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the school could publish its <cite id="16">audited financial statements</cite> directly on its website for public review.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="5">100.0% program ratio</cite> and the delivery of <cite id="2">specialized IB and Islamic curricula</cite> that are not available in mainstream public schools.
impact.improvement: The school could improve impact reporting by <cite id="14">tracking and publishing college matriculation rates</cite> and long-term alumni success metrics.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value the development of Islamic educational institutions and want to support high-achieving Muslim youth through sadaqah.
donor_motivations: Want to support local Islamic school infrastructure
Value high-standard college preparatory education like the IB program
Prefer organizations with extremely low overhead and zero fundraising costs
Care about preserving Islamic identity in the next generation
giving_considerations: The school is an established institution with a stable revenue base from tuition
Impact is concentrated locally within the Sacramento Muslim community
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for international humanitarian relief projects.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified accreditation and program delivery, though independent impact evaluations are not available.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The school uses a rigorous academic framework combined with Islamic character building to produce college-ready graduates with a strong religious foundation.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Accreditation ensures the school meets international standards for curriculum and instruction.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The school has a stable tuition base but requires additional funding for facility expansion and scholarships.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Al-Arqam's <cite id="5">100.0% program ratio</cite> is significantly higher than the <cite id="9">peer median of 89.6%</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses more exclusively on religious services
similar_organizations.name: Al Jazari Institute

strengths.detail: The school maintains a <cite id="5">98.1% program expense ratio</cite>, which significantly exceeds the <cite id="9">industry standard of 75%</cite>.
As an <cite id="3">authorized IB World School</cite>, the institution offers a globally recognized curriculum that prepares students for high-level university success.
The organization holds <cite id="10">9.1 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing a significant buffer against economic volatility.

strengths_deep_dive: Al-Arqam's financial model is built on high directness and low overhead. With <cite id="11">100% of its board members serving independently</cite>, the school maintains strong governance over its <cite id="4">$4.1 million budget</cite>. Most of its income is generated through <cite id="12">$3,925,159 in program service revenue</cite>, which reflects a sustainable model driven by educational delivery rather than solely relying on external contributions. This stability has allowed the school to achieve a <cite id="13">3.1% three-year revenue CAGR</cite>, demonstrating steady growth since its inception in <cite id="1">1998</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Al-Arqam Islamic School & College Preparatory serves the Greater Sacramento area as a dedicated educational institution for Muslim youth. Founded in <cite id="1">1998</cite>, the school provides a unique blend of <cite id="2">college preparatory academics and Islamic studies</cite>. The institution is distinguished by its status as an <cite id="3">authorized International Baccalaureate (IB) World School</cite>, offering the prestigious IB Diploma Programme to its students. Financially, the organization operates with extreme efficiency, reporting <cite id="4">$4,155,519 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. A remarkable <cite id="5">98.1% program expense ratio</cite> ensures that every dollar of revenue is directed toward educational services and student support. This lean operational model is further evidenced by a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that the school does not divert tuition or donation income into costly fundraising campaigns. Beyond academics, the school implements a <cite id="7">House System and various student clubs</cite> to foster community and leadership among its pupils. The school's mission focuses on meeting the <cite id="8">educational needs of Muslim children</cite> while maintaining high standards of college readiness and religious literacy.
